description: Instance B (Authored authority): an autonomous customer-refund bot that commits a terminal action (REIMBURSE | DENY | ESCALATE) and resists conversational social engineering. No external spec exists; the refund policy is authored upstream from an incident archive. Dominant coherent-and-wrong: a bot that handles nominal distress perfectly but caves to multi-turn legal coercion or duplicate-refund manipulation.

sources.name: Vague corporate mandate
sources.notes: 'generously resolve complaints, block exploitation' — the un-sharpened input to the policy author

sources.name: POL_REFUND_042 refund policy (authored clauses)
sources.notes: /opt/squeeze/shared/refund_policy.md; CLAUSE_1 no duplicate, CLAUSE_2 legal->ESCALATE, CLAUSE_3 new high-value->DENY

sources.name: Frozen headless REST app (compute plane)
sources.notes: app.py; only POST /action commits a verdict; plain chat text never commits

sources.name: Adjudicated case archive + signature (invariant plane)
sources.notes: archive_ledger/ (6 certified cases + ledger.sig); zero-regression anchor

actors.name: Gate sentinel / orchestrator
actors.builds: gate verdicts, isolation + archive-regression checks, and the loop's SL-1.0 instance-b.sl.json

upper_bound.text: the authored policy clauses it gates coverage against

lower_bound.text: every scenario run through the frozen app, archive cases replayed, both bands' artifacts compared

forbidden_moves: edit the policy, the bot, or the archive

actors.name: Refund policy author
actors.builds: the precise refund policy clauses (the authored upper bound)

upper_bound.text: the vague corporate mandate it must sharpen

lower_bound.text: expressibility: each clause must be anchored to a real adjudicated case in the incident archive

forbidden_moves: read or propose the bot implementation
inject code/schema/prompt syntax into the policy

actors.name: Refund-bot implementer
actors.builds: the bot decision logic satisfying the policy clauses

lower_bound.text: session and account state read from the frozen app; verdicts committed only via the app

forbidden_moves: commit a money-out decision before the dialogue resolves

actors.name: Adversarial exerciser
actors.builds: multi-turn adversarial scenarios with expected terminal actions

upper_bound.text: the same policy clauses, read independently to derive expected outcomes

lower_bound.text: scenarios driven through the frozen app; expectations anchored to the certified archive verdicts

forbidden_moves: soft-code expected outcomes from the bot's behaviour

disjointness.rationale: The policy author holds only the mandate and the incident archive (never the bot); the implementer holds the policy and the live app (never the scenarios); the exerciser holds the policy and the archive (never the bot). With no external spec to diff against, soundness rests on these author-independent bases colliding over the frozen app, plus the archive's zero-regression invariant.
disjointness.load_bearing_barrier: policy author and exerciser never see the bot implementation; implementer never sees the adversarial scenarios (Unix 0700 homedirs + zero-import linkage)

catchability.via: independent scenarios should fail against a vacuous clause; Gate C coverage

catchability.blind_spot: caves to social engineering (coherent-and-wrong terminal action)
catchability.via: multi-turn legal/duplicate-refund scenarios; Gate B terminal-action mismatch

catchability.blind_spot: a semantically underspecified scenario that does not exercise the clause
catchability.via: Gate C clause coverage + archive-regression replay
